title: Top 10 Dungeon Crawls - Part One
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=h_v3GcIgbvM

Daniel (@dungeondive) finally tackles what everyone's been asking for: his definitive ranking of top dungeon crawls. This is Part One (10-6), and Daniel's immediately setting expectations: this list is subjective, focusing specifically on games with tight corridors, character progression, and that pure dungeon-diving experience—not sprawling adventure games or traditional tabletop RPGs. The bottom half of his ranking opens with Space Hulk 4th Edition, which Daniel calls perhaps the best-produced board game he's ever seen, though its two-player skirmish nature keeps it from seeing the table as often as he'd like. Escape the Dark Castle follows as a card-based narrative gem that never fails to delight any table. Mice and Mystics captures something special with its autumnal colors and inviting atmosphere despite its timer-based tension. Mansions of Madness 2nd Edition demonstrates brilliant app integration that actually improves the experience, and Shadows of Brimstone rounds out the bottom five—an overwhelming sprawl of content that captures the pure feeling of exploration and mystery in abundance.

Daniel emphasizes that while the components might bury you, these games collectively showcase what makes dungeon crawling special: the unpredictable encounters, the thrill of tight corridors, and that sense of anything being possible when you descend into darkness.

----

Where do your personal dungeon crawl favorites fall on Daniel's scale—and which games did you expect to see higher (or at all) on this list?
